summary:
The predominant connective tissue cells in the periodontal ligament are fibroblasts, which maintain its structural integrity by producing collagen and synthesizing the extracellular matrix. \boxed{Fibroblasts}

The summary accurately captures the essential information regarding the identity of the dominant cellular component within the periodontal ligament while adhering strictly to two concise statements as per your instructions. It emphasizes their functional importance without unnecessary details or tangential content not directly related to answering the specific query posed.
